text: Every household runs on memory. Appointments. Medicines. Money. The gate you forgot to close. This is the story of a house that remembers — so its people don't have to.

Big Tech will happily rent you a listener. But your voice, your habits, your home — become their data. One family chose a different path. Build it yourself. Own every byte. And pay: nothing. Zero dollars a month.

Maya is the face and the voice. English, Hindi, or both in the same sentence. She reads the markets with a committee of analyst minds, sorts the inbox down to the three emails that matter, remembers everything you tell her — and runs the home.

But one mind alone should never be trusted with everything. So the household became a pantheon. Chakra: security. He guards the family against scammers, deep-fakes, and intrusions — and he watches Maya herself. Samay: the eternal diary. Every action, every decision, recorded forever. Tamper-proof. Narad: the messenger. Every word between agents passes through him — logged, and policed. And Krishna: counsel. He sees everything, and touches nothing. He advises. The family decides.

Morning: one brief. Markets, weather, the emails that matter. Afternoon: should I buy this stock? Four analysts — value, growth, risk, and the India specialist — debate it in seconds, and you hear the verdict, with the reasoning.

Maya, lights. Is the garage closed? The house obeys. And homes an ocean away report in: a camera in India spots motion at the gate — only the event crosses the sea, never the video. Every property, one private network. No cloud in the middle.
That caller is not your grandson. It failed the family code word. Hang up — I've already blocked the number.

Every new power the system gains must be earned — through trust tiers, promoted only on evidence. Every action lands in Samay's ledger. And if anything ever feels wrong: one button freezes it all. The kill switch lives outside the machine.

This isn't a gadget. It's a fifty-year plan. Memory that doesn't fade. Guardians that don't sleep. And decades from now, the house will still answer — in whichever language its family dreamed in last night.
Built on free tiers and open source. Nothing rented. Nothing surveilled. Zero dollars a month. Maya, and the Pantheon. A household with a mind.
